reasoning: The Lean signature encodes |G| = 105 via `[Fintype G] [Group G]` together with `card G = 105` (where `card` is `Fintype.card` thanks to `open Fintype`), matching the informal hypothesis. The conclusion `(∃ P : Sylow 5 G, P.Normal) ∧ (∃ P : Sylow 7 G, P.Normal)` says there exists a normal Sylow 5-subgroup and a normal Sylow 7-subgroup, which is exactly the standard reading of "G has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup and a normal Sylow 7-subgroup." By Sylow's theorems, having one normal Sylow p-subgroup is equivalent to all Sylow p-subgroups being normal (they are all conjugate), so the ∃-encoding carries the same content. No hypotheses are added or dropped; no types are changed. This is a faithful formalization.
